description_long: This object consists of two rustic wooden fence segments, each constructed with three thick, roughly hewn vertical posts and two horizontal rails. The upper segment is painted with a weathered white finish, showing significant wear and brown wood exposed beneath, while the lower segment is unpainted, displaying a natural brown wood texture. Both segments have a hand-crafted, irregular appearance, with uneven rail alignment and visible wood grain. The posts are chunky and rectangular, with flat tops and no decorative caps. The fence is solid and sturdy, suitable for marking boundaries in parks, gardens, or along sidewalks. There are no gates, hinges, or ornamental features present.
description: A pair of rustic wooden fence segments, one weathered white and one natural brown, each with two horizontal rails and chunky posts.
description_view_0: Front view showing both fence segments, highlighting the weathered white paint on the upper segment and natural brown wood on the lower segment.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence segments and the depth of the posts and rails.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same structure as the front, with clear visibility of the wood grain and paint wear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the rails and posts.
